_comment: F4 CS funding scanner — HIGH-confidence validation oracle. Hand-verified 2026-04-11 against snapshots in scripts/f4_snapshots/ against the tightened extraction prompt (strict HIGH requires 'computer science'/'coding'/'programming'/'CS' verbatim in snippet or title). Gate threshold: ≥60% of labeled HIGH extractions must have is_real_lea_award=true. Oracle is a point-in-time artifact — regenerate when re-verifying F4 after query/prompt revisions.

rows.notes: PAsmart competitive grant explicitly for K-12 CS + Technical Skills. Published on the district's own website. Real.
Borderline — Google.org / CS education context. CCSD 15 is a real IL district. Snippet mentions computer science education so passes strict HIGH bar.
Federal ed.gov EIR FY2023 award: 'Computer Science and Engineering and Design STEM Program and K-12 Computer Science Pathway', $694,176 / $2,171,699 total. Real.
District announcement: 'Coronado Schools Awarded $1.25 Million Computer Science Grant'. Real.
Palumbo Grant as part of PA Governor Wolf's $8.7M package to expand CS classes. Real.

Haiku mistagged the state. The underlying article is about 55 Ohio local districts (including Lake Local SD OH) winning $10.35M in Ohio Computer Science Promise grants. There is no 'Lake Public Schools' in Oklahoma. False positive at the (district, state) level even though the grant is real.
Haiku mistagged state — McKenzie Special School District is in Tennessee, not Indiana. Grant is a real $179,326 CS education award but the district/state tuple is wrong.
Haiku mistagged state — same facebook post as above, real district is TN. False (PA).
Midland ISD awarded $674,000 LASO grants including Advanced Placement Computer Science Principles (APCSP). Real.

rows.district: Northern Valley Regional High School District
rows.notes: NJ DOE official District Detail PDF: 'Awarded NJDOE: Advanced Computer Science Grant, Career Pathways CTE grant'. Real — out-of-territory but the oracle labels by truthfulness, not by territory. Territory filter is a separate downstream concern.
Rock Island-Milan SD 41 Summer 2025 Newsletter explicitly lists 'Computer Science Equity Grant' as a secured funding source. Real.
Golden State Pathways Program grant from CDE for $5.5M expanding computer science and other career pathways. Real.
$450,000 in PAsmart Advancing Grants for CTE programs expanding computer science access. Real.
District announcement: Sto-Rox SD awarded $75,000 by the Pennsylvania Department of Education for computer science + STEM programming. Real.
